title: BSNC Announces New Board Officers
summary: The BSNC Board of Directors has elected new officers. The Chairman seat was vacant due to the passing of longtime Board Chairman Henry Ivanoff Sr., who passed away on March 30, 2022. BSNC Vice Chairman Lee Ryan was elected as Chairman. BSNC also elected Cindy Towarak Massie as Vice Chairman, Eugene...

title: Camp Igaliq – Applications Open
summary: Kawerak Wellness 2022 Camp Igaliq will be July 15-20 at Camp Nuuk in Nome! Applications will be due June 23. Each summer youth from Nome and the Bering Strait region travel together to create a bright vision of themselves, their community and their future. Elders and guest presenters share knowledge...

title: Alaska Native Artist Relief Fund | Round II
summary: The Artist Relief Fund was created to provide Native artists and craftspersons who were negatively impacted by our decision to hold the 2020 and 2021 annual conventions virtually due to COVI0-19 with financial relief. The Fund was created through Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security (CARES...

title: Fifteen new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta June 10
summary: On June 10, YKHC reports fifteen new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta. title: BSNC Names Kim Cunningham as Chief Financial Officer
summary: BSNC is pleased to announce the hire of Kim Cunningham as Chief Financial Officer (CFO). Cunningham joins BSNC from NANA Regional Corporation (NANA), where she served as Senior Vice President and CFO for the corporation and its wholly-owned subsidiary, NANA Development Corporation (now Sivu, LLC). “...

title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent: June 9, 2022
summary: June 9, 2022: Over the past week, the sea ice has now decreased by a higher rate of 5.03% from the level on 06/02/2022 of 11.977 M km 2 to 11.393 M km 2 for this week. title: The Arctic This Week Take Five: Week of June 6, 2022
summary: Take Five is The Arctic Institute's weekly redux on the Arctic's most important stories, published every Friday. The post The Arctic This Week Take Five: Week of June 6, 2022 appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .

title: Twenty-three new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta June 9
